reporting-org.narrative: The Palestinian Initiative for the Promotion of Global Dialogue and Democracy

title.narrative: We Rise

description.narrative: The Project contributes to alleviate the double jeopardy faced by Palestinian women and girls due to the continued Israeli occupation and its violations of human rights and discriminatory and apartheid policies in addition to the burden of the patriarchal social norms that restrict and impose limitations on women’s rights. Furthermore, the deteriorating governance within the Palestinian Authority inflict additional burden on Palestinian Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) and women rights’ defenders. These burdens prevent the later to actively work on advancing progressive legal and policy framework that ensures a fair representation and participation of women in peace building processes.

participating-org.narrative: The Palestinian Initiative for the Promotion of Global Dialogue and Democracy

description.narrative: |Year 1 Year 1 budget

title.narrative: Output 2.2: Capacities of Security Forces are enhanced in terms of human rights principles and gender equality (UNSCR 1325 and CEDAW) to enhance women's representation in decision making positions in security forces (Y5-2025)
# of individuals (male) with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.
Not applicable for this year
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: # of individuals (female) with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: INTERMEDIATE OUTCOME 2: Increased and meaningful participation of women and girls, women rights organizations and networks, in local, national, regional and international decision-making spaces and processes (Y1-2021)

description.narrative: 2.4 # of laws, policies and strategies blocked, adopted or improved to promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protect women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations. (link SCS2), MFA basket indicator 4.1

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.

Through the project, consortium members will continue their profuse efforts in influencing the PA and duty bearers to adopt policies to increase women’s representation and political participation in reconciliation processes, local governance, national and international influencing through advocacy on amending local and national election laws and regulations. This influence will be exerted through gender review of spending/policy procedures of a number of governmental ministers, demand/policy papers, factsheets, media productions, organizing online and offline accountability sessions with decision makers in addition to other advocacy activities; Fostering policy and social dialogue on the decision-makers level and the community level on the importance of women political participation and representation in terms of awareness-raising.
Not applicable for this year

description.narrative: # of laws, policies and strategies blocked, adopted or improved to promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protect women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations. (link SCS2) MFA Basket indicator 4.1

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.
Not applicable for this year
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: # of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement-building at subnational level

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics.

Consortium partner MIFTAH held two policy sessions: 1) an emergency session for several CSOs and representatives of national parties and factions to discuss local elections following the September 6 Cabinet decision which calls for holding local council elections in two stages. As a result, Civil society institutions presented a number of their demands in a policy paper which included their rejection of the Cabinet’s decision. The policy session concluded with an agreement to continue coordination with the political parties and to continue pressuring the government to issue a decision to hold one-time local council elections in accordance with the democratic values of transparency and human rights. They also called for avoiding the political challenges that hinder elections in the Gaza Strip or prevent CSOs from assuming their oversight role on monitoring elections. 2) Another policy session was held in Gaza Strip during October 2021 to continue the advocacy efforts for holding the local elections during the first quarter of 2022 nationally on one ballot day including Gaza Strip and West Bank. The meeting opened the door for a national dialogue to include the demanded amendments that guarantee increased participation and representation of women and youth and enhanced pluralism and inclusion of all in the electoral process

title.narrative: # of individuals (female) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: In 2021, the pressure and smearing campaigns against Human rights activists’ and Palestinian CSOs have amplified. Some CSOs were attacked and smeared by the GoI (such as the case of the six Palestinian CSOs), and some CSOs activists were arrested by the GoI and PA, and in some instances killed. This imposes huge pressure on the democratic processes and free space for civic socity. There are only few available tools to CSO to allow for a greater accountability for the violations of the HR and IHL in the oPt. Some of these tools are the documentation of these violations; and access to the international fora and platforms to present these documentations to the international community, and thus holding the government of Israel (GoI) and the Palestinian Authority accountable for the harm and pressure exerted on Palestinian civilians and HR activists.

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics.
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: # of individuals (male) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.

Project partners will continue working profusely with the communities in the West Bank and Gaza Strip to raise their awareness on issues related to elections and democratic processes. Project aspire to achieve these results through delivering awareness sessions and town-hall meetings for women and university students on electoral laws and regulations, citizenship, the peaceful transfer of authority in different areas of the oPt. Furthermore, project partners will be working with universities and feminist organizations to organize for youth led initiatives in the WB and Gaza strip. The project aims to have the youth as change agents to champion and promote women' meaningful and equal participation across their communities.
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: Cross-cutting (Y2-2022)
# of women led CSOs included in SPs programmes

description.narrative: MFA Basket indicator #6

comment.narrative: Will be reported on in due time

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.
Not applicable for this year

MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics. comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.

Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.

Project partners will continue working profusely with the communities in the West Bank and Gaza Strip to raise their awareness on issues related to elections and democratic processes. Project aspire to achieve these results through delivering awareness sessions and town-hall meetings for women and university students on electoral laws and regulations, citizenship, the peaceful transfer of authority in different areas of the oPt. comment.narrative: In 2021, the pressure and smearing campaigns against Human rights activists’ and Palestinian CSOs have amplified. Some CSOs were attacked and smeared by the GoI (such as the case of the six Palestinian CSOs), and some CSOs activists were arrested by the GoI and PA, and in some instances killed. This imposes huge pressure on the democratic processes and free space for civic socity. There are only few available tools to CSO to allow for a greater accountability for the violations of the HR and IHL in the oPt. Some of these tools are the documentation of these violations; and access to the international fora and platforms to present these documentations to the international community, and thus holding the government of Israel (GoI) and the Palestinian Authority accountable for the harm and pressure exerted on Palestinian civilians and HR activists.

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics.

(link SCS2), MFA basket indicator 4.1

comment.narrative: In 2021, the pressure and smearing campaigns against Human rights activists’ and Palestinian CSOs have amplified. Some CSOs were attacked and smeared by the GoI (such as the case of the six Palestinian CSOs), and some CSOs activists were arrested by the GoI and PA, and in some instances killed. non youth
Will be reported on later

title.narrative: # of individuals (male) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.

Project partners will continue working profusely with the communities in the West Bank and Gaza Strip to raise their awareness on issues related to elections and democratic processes. Project aspire to achieve these results through delivering awareness sessions and town-hall meetings for women and university students on electoral laws and regulations, citizenship, the peaceful transfer of authority in different areas of the oPt. Furthermore, project partners will be working with universities and feminist organizations to organize for youth led initiatives in the WB and Gaza strip. The project aims to have the youth as change agents to champion and promote women' meaningful and equal participation across their communities. (youth)
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: # of individuals (male) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.
non youth

title.narrative: # of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement-building at subnational level

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics. (1 formal)
will be reported on in due time

title.narrative: # of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women
indirect formal

title.narrative: INTERMEDIATE OUTCOME 2: Increased and meaningful participation of women and girls, women rights organizations and networks, in local, national, regional and international decision-making spaces and processes (Y5-2025)

# of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement-building at subnational level

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics. (1 formal)
will be reported on in due time

title.narrative: # of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women
indirect formal

title.narrative: # of individuals (male) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

title.narrative: # of individuals (female) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.
Will be reported on later

description.narrative: # of laws, policies and strategies blocked, adopted or improved to promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protect women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations. (link SCS2) MFA Basket indicator 4.1

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.
1 law and/or policy
Not applicable for this year

description.narrative: 2.4 # of laws, policies and strategies blocked, adopted or improved to promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protect women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations. (link SCS2), MFA basket indicator 4.1

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.

Through the project, consortium members will continue their profuse efforts in influencing the PA and duty bearers to adopt policies to increase women’s representation and political participation in reconciliation processes, local governance, national and international influencing through advocacy on amending local and national election laws and regulations. This influence will be exerted through gender review of spending/policy procedures of a number of governmental ministers, demand/policy papers, factsheets, media productions, organizing online and offline accountability sessions with decision makers in addition to other advocacy activities; Fostering policy and social dialogue on the decision-makers level and the community level on the importance of women political participation and representation in terms of awareness-raising.

title.narrative: Cross-cutting (Y1-2021)
# of women led CSOs included in SPs programmes

description.narrative: MFA Basket indicator #6

comment.narrative: Building on its ongoing partnership with the Palestinian women's coalition for the Implementation of UNSCR 1325, consortium partner MIFTAH contributed to the development of a specialized manual on UN mechanisms (Contractual and Non-Contractual) related to UNSCR 1325 and subsequent resolutions, in addition to CEDAW convention and its General Recommendations (30, 32) to support the 20 feminist CSO members of Palestinian Women Coalition (PWC) in their advocacy work. The manual sets out international tools and platforms for better influencing and directing the advocacy efforts at the international level for the realization of peace and security for Palestinian women in an independent sovereign state of Palestine. The manual also supports advocacy efforts towards holding perpetrators accountable for the extended violence and military attacks and war crimes against Palestinians by the Israeli occupation. The manual supports the coalition members in utilizing the UN mechanisms through written information/ statements, attending sessions, providing oral statements …etc., and more importantly, it will enable the coalition members in following a clear agenda and dates for key events and sessions that will serve their advocacy efforts at international platforms. Furthermore, the manual will facilitate the PWC work in addressing the Secretary-General, the Security Council, the General Assembly, the Economic and Social Council, CSW, the Human Rights Council. Member CSOs will be further trained on how to make best use of the manual.

title.narrative: Output 3.2.: National Coalition UNSCR 1325 is supported to develop and implement the Advocacy strategy (Y2-2022)
# of women led CSOs with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 3.2.1 # of organizations (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality (link SCS5) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.1

comment.narrative: The national coalition consists of a group of CSOs, working together towards advancing the 1325 NAP. Historically, The national 1325 coalition has tremendously beneficiated from the strategic partnership and support of the WPS consortium members. They contributed towards developing the advocacy strategies and plans of the coalitions. The coalition highly depends of the contributions of WPS consortium partners for its development.

title.narrative: Output 3.2.: National Coalition UNSCR 1325 is supported to develop and implement the Advocacy strategy (Y3-2023)
# of women led CSOs with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality

comment.narrative: The national coalition consists of a group of CSOs, working together towards advancing the 1325 NAP. Historically, The national 1325 coalition has tremendously beneficiated from the strategic partnership and support of the WPS consortium members. They contributed towards developing the advocacy strategies and plans of the coalitions. The coalition highly depends of the contributions of WPS consortium partners for its development.

title.narrative: Output 3.2.: National Coalition UNSCR 1325 is supported to develop and implement the Advocacy strategy (Y4-2024)
# of women led CSOs with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 3.2.1 # of organizations (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality (link SCS5) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.1

comment.narrative: The national coalition consists of a group of CSOs, working together towards advancing the 1325 NAP. Historically, The national 1325 coalition has tremendously beneficiated from the strategic partnership and support of the WPS consortium members. They contributed towards developing the advocacy strategies and plans of the coalitions. The coalition highly depends of the contributions of WPS consortium partners for its development.

title.narrative: INTERMEDIATE OUTCOME 2: Increased and meaningful participation of women and girls, women rights organizations and networks, in local, national, regional and international decision-making spaces and processes (Y2-2022)

# of individuals (male) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: While the Palestinian society is a young society, youth remain indifferent to issues relating to political participation in general and to women's participation in particular. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Moreover, there are a sense of hopelessness and fatigue among young women and men towards achieving a real positive change in the political establishment.

Project partners will continue working profusely with the communities in the West Bank and Gaza Strip to raise their awareness on issues related to elections and democratic processes. Project aspire to achieve these results through delivering awareness sessions and town-hall meetings for women and university students on electoral laws and regulations, citizenship, the peaceful transfer of authority in different areas of the oPt. Furthermore, project partners will be working with universities and feminist organizations to organize for youth led initiatives in the WB and Gaza strip. The project aims to have the youth as change agents to champion and promote women' meaningful and equal participation across their communities.
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: # of individuals (female) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations

description.narrative: 2.6 # of individuals (disaggregated by type, age and gender) with improved attitudes and practices on promoting women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention, peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-) conflict situations MFA Basket indicator 4.3

comment.narrative: In 2021, the pressure and smearing campaigns against Human rights activists’ and Palestinian CSOs have amplified. Some CSOs were attacked and smeared by the GoI (such as the case of the six Palestinian CSOs), and some CSOs activists were arrested by the GoI and PA, and in some instances killed. This imposes huge pressure on the democratic processes and free space for civic socity. There are only few available tools to CSO to allow for a greater accountability for the violations of the HR and IHL in the oPt. Some of these tools are the documentation of these violations; and access to the international fora and platforms to present these documentations to the international community, and thus holding the government of Israel (GoI) and the Palestinian Authority accountable for the harm and pressure exerted on Palestinian civilians and HR activists.

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics.
Will be updated next year

title.narrative: # of times that CSOs succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement-building at subnational level

description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

The project aims to allow CSO the platforms and opportunities to succeed in voicing their demands and positions. This will be done through organizing policy dialogue sessions among CSOs on annual basis to further push for the adoption of proposed amendments on national elections; radio talk shows to foster social dialogue on a number of topics including local elections, women representation in security forces and other relevant topics.
will be reported on in due time

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.

comment.narrative: The laws in the oPt are yet to be developed to allow greater access of women to their rights of meaningful participation. For example, electoral law related to the gender quota remains at 20% for women's representation. However, many women are still significantly underrepresented in leadership positions and in political public spheres in general. Baseline has shown that only one women out of 10 is being represented or is participating in the public life. CSOs have been demanding for a fair increase to the current quota system in order to ensure meaningful participation for women and encourage women's role in leading electoral lists.

Through the project, consortium members will continue their profuse efforts in influencing the PA and duty bearers to adopt policies to increase women’s representation and political participation in reconciliation processes, local governance, national and international influencing through advocacy on amending local and national election laws and regulations. This influence will be exerted through gender review of spending/policy procedures of a number of governmental ministers, demand/policy papers, factsheets, media productions, organizing online and offline accountability sessions with decision makers in addition to other advocacy activities; Fostering policy and social dialogue on the decision-makers level and the community level on the importance of women political participation and representation in terms of awareness-raising.

Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. Youth are also highly impacted by the patriarchal norms and traditions that are prevalent in the Palestinian society. Youth have been excluded from public spheres and have developed a significant mistrust of the current political system/establishment due to the lack of democratic and transparent electoral processes. description.narrative: 2.5 # of times that CSOs (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) succeed in creating space for CSO demands and positions on promote women’s meaningful and equal participation and leadership in conflict prevention and peace- and state-building and protecting women’s and girls’ rights in crisis and (post-)conflict situations, through agenda setting, influencing the debate and/or movement building (link SCS3). MFA Basket indicator 4.2

comment.narrative: While many CSOs have exerted efforts in creating a space to highlight CSOs demands and discussions on women's meaningful participation, it is evident through the baseline study (2021) that only a limited number of these efforts were successful. Women' participation in public jobs in the civil sector was 44.4% in 2018, while women who occupied a “general manager” post and higher was only 13.1% .Figure 20 shows women representation in the various areas of the public positions, . In general, women participation is very limited, and in best cases didn’t exceed 21%, which is alarming and requires intervention. Women highest participation is in student councils while they have zero representation in unions, and pale representation in political parties. In the past three years, the Palestinian Authority (PA) has been exerting pressure to limit and restrict the work and influence of CSOs in the oPt through introducing policies and procedures that limit and restrain the free space for CBOs to fully expressing their positions and demands. This reflects a need to intervene at such levels to raise awareness of such bodies, and push them to adopt more open and encouraging policies that is more supportive and inclusive for women

title.narrative: Output 3.2.: National Coalition UNSCR 1325 is supported to develop and implement the Advocacy strategy (Y5-2025)
# of women led CSOs with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 3.2.1 # of organizations (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality (link SCS5) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.1

comment.narrative: The national coalition consists of a group of CSOs, working together towards advancing the 1325 NAP. Historically, The national 1325 coalition has tremendously beneficiated from the strategic partnership and support of the WPS consortium members. They contributed towards developing the advocacy strategies and plans of the coalitions. The coalition highly depends of the contributions of WPS consortium partners for its development.
Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: Output 2.2: Capacities of Security Forces are enhanced in terms of human rights principles and gender equality (UNSCR 1325 and CEDAW) to enhance women's representation in decision making positions in security forces (Y2-2022)
# of individuals (male) with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.
30 local authorities, non-youth
Will be updated in due time

title.narrative: # of individuals (female) with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.
10 local authorities, non-youth
Will be updated in due time

title.narrative: Output 2.2: Capacities of Security Forces are enhanced in terms of human rights principles and gender equality (UNSCR 1325 and CEDAW) to enhance women's representation in decision making positions in security forces (Y3-2023)
# of individuals (male) with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.

Not applicable for this year

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.

description.narrative: 2.2.1 # of individuals with strengthened capacity (knowledge and skills) to advance women’s rights and gender equality (disaggregated by type, age and gender) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.2

comment.narrative: Through partners work and building on earlier assessments, it was observed that most of the security forces are not aware of the CEDAW convention or have negative perceptions of its applications due to the spread of misinformation and ill-founded discourse among some of the Palestinian cities on the CEDAW convention. Security forces are also predominantly hypermasculine men and lack capacities to understand and respond effectively to women's rights and gender equality issues. Moreover, security forces are not provided with well-structured training on Gender equality and women’s rights, and that might leave a gap on their practices when approaching women and girls. E.g security forces used excessive force against female demonstrators in Ramallah city during 2021 demonstrations, which reinforces the hypothesis that security forces need more understanding of women’s rights, and that become aware about the instruments that protect women from violence.

Not applicable for this year

title.narrative: Output 3.2.: National Coalition UNSCR 1325 is supported to develop and implement the Advocacy strategy (Y1-2021)
# of women led CSOs with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality

description.narrative: 3.2.1 # of organizations (disaggregated by women-led, youth-led or other and formal/informal) with strengthened capacity to advance women’s rights and gender equality (link SCS5) MFA Basket indicator 5.2.1

comment.narrative: The national coalition consists of a group of CSOs, working together towards advancing the 1325 NAP. Historically, The national 1325 coalition has tremendously beneficiated from the strategic partnership and support of the WPS consortium members. They contributed towards developing the advocacy strategies and plans of the coalitions. The coalition highly depends of the contributions of WPS consortium partners for its development.

Building on its ongoing partnership with the Palestinian women's coalition for the Implementation of UNSCR 1325, MIFTAH contributed to the development of a specialized manual on UN mechanisms (Contractual and Non-Contractual) related to UNSCR 1325 and subsequent resolutions, in addition to CEDAW convention and its General Recommendations (30, 32) to support the 20 feminist CSO members of Palestinian Women Coalition (PWC) in their advocacy work. The manual sets out international tools and platforms for better influencing and directing the advocacy efforts at the international level for the realization of peace and security for Palestinian women in an independent sovereign state of Palestine. The manual also supports advocacy efforts towards holding perpetrators accountable for the extended violence and military attacks and war crimes against Palestinians by the Israeli occupation.
The manual supports the coalition members in utilizing the UN mechanisms through written information/ statements, attending sessions, providing oral statements …etc., and more importantly, it will enable the coalition members in following a clear agenda and dates for key events and sessions that will serve their advocacy efforts at international platforms. Furthermore, the manual will facilitate the PWC work in addressing the Secretary-General, the Security Council, the General Assembly, the Economic and Social Council, CSW, the Human Rights Council. Member CSOs will be further trained on how to make best use of the manual.
